title: Savory Argentinian Chicken Stir-fry
description: A dense and salty meal-prep Argentinean dish featuring Cajun seasoning, dumplings, and a paleo-friendly twist.
instructions: Season the chicken with Cajun seasoning and place in a baking dish. Bake for 25 minutes or until fully cooked.
In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Add garlic and onion, sautéing for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Add sliced bell peppers to the skillet. Cook for another 3-4 minutes until they start to soften slightly.
Remove chicken from oven and set aside. In the same skillet, add the dumplings and stir for about 2-3 minutes allowing them to absorb flavors.

Place the cooked dumplings back in the baking dish with the chicken. Return the skillet to the stove-top, add a little bit of water if needed and bring to a boil for about 1 minute until slightly thickened.
Pour the sauce over the chicken and dumplings, ensuring all ingredients are well combined.
Serve hot as a main course or side dish.
